You learn how to become 2x more productive by leveraging AI | TOP NEWS "Could a $6 billion plan solve some of Texas ' water supply problems?” San Antonio Express-News' Lit Teitz \-- "As fast-growing cities, utilities and businesses clamor for more water in drought-stricken Central Texas, an ambitious $6 billion plan is emerging to meet some of their demand. The Guadalupe-Blanco River Authority wants to build a new reservoir to hold water from the river, and construct more than 250 miles of pipeline to move that water throughout the river basin, which runs from Kerr County to the Gulf Coast. Dubbed the “WaterSecure Project,” officials say the plan would primarily rely on existing water rights that the authority already has for surface water from Guadalupe River, but it could potentially incorporate aquifer storage and brackish water desalination. » If it becomes a reality, the first water from the project would be delivered in 2033, with more available by the 2040s as the project is expanded, the river authority said. It expects utilities throughout the region to participate, purchasing water and paying back bonds issued for the project. The project could involve a new reservoir in Calhoun County, a new treatment plant, new desalination plant and underground storage wells in Gonzales County, and pipelines running all the way from the new reservoir to Kendall, Comal and Hays counties, according to regional water planning documents. » It would be the latest large infrastructure project aiming to move water around the region, and likely the most expensive to date, with an estimated price tag more than double the cost of San Antonio’s Vista Ridge pipeline. In recent years, the Guadalupe-Blanco River Authority and other utilities have also spent hundreds of millions of dollars building their own pipelines to bring in groundwater from the Carrizo Aquifer east of Interstate 35 to shore up their supplies, but the WaterSecure project would be the first large-scale system aimed at transporting surface water throughout Central Texas. “We’ve consistently seen explosive growth in this region,” saidCharlie Hickman , the river authority’s executive manager of engineering. “That drives a need for new water supply for the region, and our WaterSecure is a regional project that will help fill in a portion of the future projected demands.”" ($) | PLEASE NOTE : You are receiving the PAID subscriber version of Must Read Texas FOR TODAY ONLY — so you can see what you’re missing. » Become a PAID subscriber here: . | #TXLEGE "Texas House advances bill requiring A/C in prisons; proposal 's fate uncertain in Senate” Austin American-Statesman's Bayliss Wagner \-- "The Texas House overwhelmingly passed legislation Thursday night to require air conditioning across the state’s prison system, marking the third time in five years that the lower chamber has approved such a measure. The Senate has declined to take it up in the previous two sessions. The 89-43 vote comes less than two months after an Austin federal judge and warned lawmakers that he expects to order the state to install permanent air-conditioning systemwide. It also took place hours before a midnight cutoff for the House to pass bills originating in that chamber. is expected to face an uphill battle in the more conservative state Senate, which has repeatedly declined to hear similar proposals. Democratic state Rep.Terry Canale s of Edinburg, a criminal defense attorney, said he filed the bill because the constant, sweltering heat in lockups is “inhumane” and constitutes cruel and unusual punishment. “Many people are not violent offenders,” he told the American-Statesman. “They surely didn't get sentenced to death. But we're killing them. We're cooking people.” Over 60% of Texas inmates are being held on violent offenses, 15% on drug-related offenses and 9% on property offenses. The average prisoner is 41 years old, Texas Department of Criminal Justice Director Bryan Collier has in three inmates’ deaths from multiple causes in 2023. also continue to fall ill from heat exposure, and the system struggled with a , one of the hottest years in recent Texas history. HB 3006 outlines that would require TDCJ to install climate control in one-third of its facilities by 2028, another one-third by 2030 and the final third of the facilities by 2032 — but only if it receives the funds to do so. The agency would be mandated to solicit competitive bids from private contractors to complete the project, and the cost would be capped at $100 million per phase. Canales described visiting the minimum-security prison in his hometown on a broiling Texas summer day, where the odor of male sweat is so strong that “you can taste it” and where prisoners flood their cells with toilet water, then “take turns lying in it” overnight to cool down. In a yearslong legal battle over the climate control in Texas prisons, plaintiffs are asking the court to require the state to maintain cell temperatures between 65 and 85 degrees, a similar range as and federal prisons are required to maintain. Nearly 70% of cells in the state prison system lack air conditioning, in the lawsuit. On the state's current trajectory, it would take at least 25 years to ensure all cells are climate-controlled, which is "insufficient under the Eight Amendment," U.S. District Judge Robert Pitman found in his March ruling on a request for a preliminary injunction. Pitman urged Collier to prepare for an adverse final ruling that would require Texas to air-condition all cells, and he recently scheduled a jury trial for March 2026. nearly 48,000 "cool beds" in its system so far and is in the process of procuring 12,000 more. The agency received $85 million for additional air conditioning installation in 2023 and is requesting another $118 million for the next bieennium, which it says would allow for 16,000 more air-conditioned beds. That would bring the total number to 78,000 in a system that housed in 2023. Even if a bill requiring A/C does not pass the Senate, it is likely that the prison system will receive more state funds to install air conditioning. State Sen. Joan Huffman , R-Houston, that the state budget will include the $118 million the TDCJ has requested to install around 11,000 additional cool beds over the next two years. The state will also allocate $3 million for new dorms, which would be air conditioned, she wrote in the statement, the Tribune reported. Huffman did not respond to Statesman requests for comment." ($) | STATE GOVERNMENT "Texas foster care lawsuit appealed to Supreme Court ,” Texas Tribune's Eleanor Klibanoff \-- "A long-running lawsuit against Texas’ foster care system may get another look after lawyers representing children in state care asked the U.S. Supreme Court to intervene. The petition, filed Monday, asks the high court to review from the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals that overturned a contempt order against the state and removed the judge who had overseen the case to this point. U.S. District JudgeJanis Jack was removed from the case in October, after more than 13 years as the state’s . She issued the original ruling in the 2011 lawsuit that found kids were leaving state care more damaged than when they entered, after being “shuttled throughout a system where rape, abuse, psychotropic medication, and instability are the norm.” She placed the state system under federal oversight, which the state fought in court for many years. In the years since that initial ruling, Jack found the state in contempt three times for failing to comply with court ordered reforms. The most recent contempt order was in April 2024, when she ordered the state to pay $100,000 a day until it could prove it was properly investigating abuse and neglect allegations among the most disabled children in state care. The state appealed that ruling. In October, the 5th Circuit Jack’s contempt order, ruling that it was criminal, not civil in nature, and ordered her removed from the case. Jack has been replaced by U.S. Chief District Judge Randy Crane." "Ken Paxton hit with two lawsuits over county oversight rule ,” Texas Tribune's Ayden Runnels \-- "Five Texas district attorneys are suing Attorney General Ken Paxton in two separate lawsuits filed Friday over new rules that would give the state’s top law enforcer meticulous access to their office’s records. The two lawsuits, filed in Travis County District Court, seek to overturn a new rule created by Paxton’s office giving the attorney general office’s employees discretion to request almost all documents from cases county officials work on, regardless of whether they are being pursued. The district attorneys suing Paxton said the rule is an unconstitutional overreach that would needlessly burden offices who would have to present “terabytes” of data to the attorney general. The rule, which took effect in April, only applies to counties with 400,000 residents or more — a threshold only 13 counties in the state meets. Paxton’s office has marked the provision as a way to “rein in rogue district attorneys” refusing to uphold the law. District attorneys from Travis and El Paso counties filed one suit, while district attorneys from Harris, Dallas and Bexar counties filed another. Both seek to block Paxton from being able to enforce the rule, alleging it violates the state constitution and federal law. The background: The rule, known as Chapter 56, was originally proposed in the administrative code in September 2024. It requires district attorneys to provide all documents or communications produced or received by district attorneys’ offices, including confidential information. Included in the rule’s definition of “case file” materials eligible for review are all documents, correspondence and handwritten notes relevant to a case. It also requires counties to submit quarterly reports to the attorney general on subjects, including specific information on indictments of police officers or for violations of election code. The new Chapter 56 cites a prompting district and county attorneys to report information to the attorney general “in the form that the attorney general directs.” To enforce the collection of documents and communication, the rule would create an “oversight advisory committee” composed of employees from the attorney general’s office. The committee would be able to request entire case files from district attorneys at their discretion. Failing to provide the requested documentation to the advisory committee would result in “official misconduct” under the rule, allowing a district judge to remove a district attorney from office. Why the district attorneys sued: The two lawsuits both claim the law cited by the new Chapter 56 does not provide Paxton’s office with the sweeping jurisdiction the rule creates — and that providing the information requested would be both expensive and illegal. One lawsuit from Dallas, Harris and Bexar county attorneys claims the rule seeks to achieve a “political objective” by burdening officials and creating strict consequences for noncompliance. “These reporting requirements do not make communities safer,” Bexar County District AttorneyJoe Gonzales said. “They do not identify trends, improve transparency, or enhance public trust. Instead, they create barriers that divert limited resources away from what matters most, which is prosecuting violent offenders and protecting our community.” The trio’s lawsuit also maintains the rule violates the Texas Constitution’s protections on separation of powers because the attorney general has “no authority” to expand the definition of official misconduct. The second lawsuit filed by the district attorneys from El Paso and Travis counties marks similar issues with the new rule, and also claims it would require illegally forfeiting the private information of victims working with their offices. In a press release from March, Paxton’s office states the new rule will help “assist citizens” in judging prosecutors’ performance, which attorneys in the second suit worry indicates private information could be shared with the public. “The Challenged Rules purport to require an unprecedented level of disclosure of privileged and confidential information from only some of the State’s prosecutors for the sole purpose of unconstitutional oversight,” the lawsuit reads. What Paxton says: The attorney general has lauded the new rule as a way to help the public better understand how their local prosecutors are operating, and create consequences for those who do not act. In a statement to the Texas Tribune about the lawsuit, Paxton called the rule a “straightforward, common-sense measure” that aims to shed light on attorneys who may be refusing to prosecute dangerous crimes. "It is no surprise that rogue DAs who would rather turn violent criminals loose on the streets than do their jobs are afraid of transparency and accountability,” Paxton said in a statement about the lawsuit from Dallas, Harris and Bexar county officials. “This lawsuit is meritless and merely a sad, desperate attempt to conceal information from the public they were sworn to protect." Paxton’s office also waived concerns about potentially burdensome time or financial costs, stating in the Texas Register that their assessment finds “minimal, if any, fiscal impact.”" | TEXANS IN DC “Austin Republican Chip Roy helps block Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill, ” Dallas Morning News' Joseph Morton — "U.S. Rep. Chip Roy , R-Austin, is back in a familiar position as he and fellow hold up their own party’s agenda to push for more significant spending cuts. Roy and several like-minded GOP colleagues on the House Budget Committee joined Democrats on Friday to vote down the “One Big Beautiful Bill,” which is the vehicle for policies backed by President Donald Trump and Republicans. Before the vote, Roy defended many of the bill’s provisions against Democratic attacks that it would to fund tax cuts for billionaires. Roy said hard-working Americans would benefit from many of the package’s tax provisions, funding for border security would repair damage caused by former PresidentJoe Biden’s administration and more money for defense would boost the military. He then unloaded on Republicans for going soft, however, when it comes to addressing runaway spending in programs such as Medicaid. “I have to now admonish my colleagues on this side of the aisle. This bill falls profoundly short,” Roy said. “It does not do what we say it does with respect to deficits.” He said the bill is structured to front load big deficit increases over the next few years and backload projected spending cuts in the years following. Overhauling Medicaid would go a long way toward addressing the country’s fiscal woes, he said, but the bill doesn’t do enough on that front. New Medicaid work requirements, for example, wouldn’t kick in until 2029, after Trump leaves office. Deficit hawks say that delays savings and means the provisions might never be implemented. “Only in Washington are we expected to bet on the come that in five years, then everything will work, then we will solve the problem,” Roy said. “We have got to change the direction of this town.” Roy has deep knowledge about how Washington and Congress operate, having worked for Texas’ Republican U.S. Sens. John Cornyn and Ted Cruz. He has used that former-staffer knowledge to be a thorn in the side of leadership as he advocates for conservative positions and battles what he derides as budget gimmicks that result in the national debt continuing to climb. It’s also not the first time Roy has found himself at odds with Trump." ($) | BUSINESS NEWS “SpaceX will launch more rockets from South Texas ,” Texas Tribune's Berenice Garcia — "The Federal Aviation Administration will allow SpaceX to launch rockets in South Texas up to 25 times per year, a significant increase from the five launches the company was previously licensed to complete annually. The FAA approved some modifications for the Starship Flight 9, the company’s next rocket, stopping short of full approval for a launch, it was announced Thursday. The news is the latest development forElon Musk’s space company, which has used the Rio Grande Valley beach to test its rockets since 2013. Earlier this month, residents near the SpaceX launch site — largely SpaceX employees — voted to incorporate their neighborhoods into a new city, Starbase. Cameron County is expected to sign the order to officially incorporate the city on Tuesday. Musk is also expected to be in South Texas next week. "I will give a company talk explaining the Mars game plan in Starbase, Texas, that will also be live-streamed on X," Musk wrote on his social media platform. SpaceX cannot move forward with its ninth test flight until the FAA allows it to resume test flights following the mishap of Starship Flight 8 on March 6. The launch test ended with the destruction of the starship vehicle about 10 minutes after launch. An investigation into the mishap is ongoing. Because of that mishap, the FAA is expanding the aircraft and maritime hazard areas in the U.S. and other countries. The safety measure is also because SpaceX will be reusing a previously launched Super Heavy booster rocket for the first time. Last week, the FAA paved the way for an increase in launches, finding in an environmental assessment that there would be no significant environmental impacts caused by allowing SpaceX to increase launches at their Boca Chica site from five to 25 times per year." “Taiwanese GlobalWafers opens $3.5 billion factory in Sherman, Texas ,” via AP — "Taiwanese silicon wafer manufacturer GlobalWafers said Friday it would invest an additional $4 billion in the United States to meet growing customer demand there, as it officially opened its new $3.5 billion wafer facility in Texas. “Our U.S. customers appear to have a very strong demand for U.S.-based production capacity,” Doris Hsu , chairperson of GlobalWafers, a TSMC supplier, told reporters. “Although tariffs (on semiconductors) have not been finalized...(U.S.) customers are hoping to secure local supply to reduce the potential uncertainties that tariffs may bring,” Hsu said. The plant, GlobalWafers’ most advanced fully integrated silicon wafer facility, is the first of its kind built in the U.S. in more than two decades and is currently the only advanced wafer manufacturing site in the country. Silicon wafers are critical components in chip manufacturing and larger wafers are widely used in advanced chip production as they allow for more chips to be produced per each wafer, increasing cost savings. At the opening ceremony held in Sherman, Texas, on Thursday, the company said it intends to add two more phases to the site to boost production capacity. It currently has one phase completed for production and a second phase is planned for construction in the 142-acre campus that is designed to accommodate up to six phases. Hsu said there is no set timeline for the additional two phases, but several conditions must be met before moving forward. “Phases one and two must be profitable, and we need to secure customers... who show strong interest in local production and are willing to sign long-term contracts,” Hsu said. “We also need reasonable pricing, prepayments, and government support. If these conditions are met, we’ll move ahead.” The plant is part of the Biden administration-era CHIPS for America program and the company is set to receive $406 million in U.S. government grants for its projects in Texas and Missouri. Hsu said it has not yet received the U.S. funding but has reached key milestones and submitted the required documentation. Hsu expected the funds to be disbursed in the first half of this year." | NEWS FROM AROUND THE STATE DMN: "What to know about the Supreme Court arguments in the birthright citizenship case " THE TEXAN: "Bill Decriminalizing Homosexual Conduct Passes Texas House " KXAN: "House approves bill to remove Texas ‘homosexual conduct’ ban " THE TEXAN: "At a Premium: Texas Legislature Mulls Reforms to Cumbersome Insurance Industry " HOU CHRON: "Why Ted Cruz is so worried about Qatar giving Trump a luxury jet " THE TEXAN: "Texas House Approves Enhanced 'Junk Science Writ' Following Roberson Controversy" COMMUNITY IMPACT: "Texas Senate panel unveils amended version of $8B school finance proposal " THE TEXAN: "Abbott Requests Waiver to Exempt 'Junk Food' from SNAP Program" THE TEXAN: "The 40: Farm Bill, Renewables Tax Credit, Investment Accounts, Reconciliation " THE TEXAN: "Weekly Roundup: House Deadline, Lottery Commission Abolition, STAAR Test Eliminated " KXAN: "Abbott asks USDA to prohibit ‘unhealthy foods’ from SNAP benefits " SAEN: "Trump 's 'beautiful' bill spans 1,116 pages. name: Jeffrey Lichtenstein, OnePA
subject: Tomorrow’s election is a big deal.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by One Pennsylvania
body:
Here’s what it matters, and what we’re doing to get the job done. | [ ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107989272/546491485/-551885580?refcode=EM_FL_OPA_20250519_fr_explain_fd_pb_act_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vQ1BELzEvMTEzNTk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQyZGI5OGQzLWU3MzQtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ByRiU1AY8Lr9Bpa7eKU1efCEJcgNT2s0It8q4O1QWZo=)
---
Tomorrow’s election is a big deal. Let us explain. **Everyone everywhere is watching to see whether or not progressives can win in the face of Trump’s billionaire takeover.** Since everything is under attack from Trump’s authoritarian regime, **local and state leaders are our first and best line of defense.** Some in the democratic coalition want to play it safe with centrist politicians and tepid plans, but those rooted in the community know our people want courageous opposition to MAGA and bold policy solutions. That’s why we’ve made winning these primaries to defend our progressive local champions a critical priority. Here’s a look at what we’re up against and the work we’re doing to get the job done. _**Philadelphia District Attorney Larry Krasner**_ Larry Krasner’s re-election is about more than one office—it’s a fight for the soul of our justice system. He’s made the **system fairer, held powerful people accountable, and helped build real safety without destroying lives.**
* Philadelphia’s gun violence and homicide rates have **dropped steadily in the last five years**. At the same time, total sentenced years **is down by half**.
* Krasner has vowed to continue **standing up to MAGA**. Last year he **filed suit against Elon Musk** for illegally giving $1,000,000 checks to Pennsylvanians who signed up to vote for Trump.
* Krasner has also protected **free speech and the right to protest** , even as Trump has tried to curb our rights. He has also built a robust **Immigration Counsel Unit** , to protect vulnerable communities.
* Last, programs like **juvenile diversion, substance use diversion and mental health diversion** and steering young people away from lifelong system involvement and **helping thousands get treatment instead of jail time**.

**Re-electing Larry Krasner is a top priority. Here’s what we’re doing to get the job done.** **First:** One PA built a community-empowered organizing effort with 15 Precinct Captains in the most important precincts in Philly. **Then:** We built a high quality door-to-door campaign to reach voters in the 10 most important wards. **Finally:** We are reaching voters online, by text and over the phone, making sure they know Larry Krasner kept Philadelphians safe, how to return their mail in ballots, and where to vote on election day. _**Pittsburgh Mayor Ed Gainey**_ Four years ago, Mayor Gainey made history by being sworn in as the first Black mayor of Pittsburgh and he got to work. **First:** he pushed for his bold agenda to bring down housing costs, fight climate change at the city level, and transform city policing with evidence-informed approaches to keep us safe. **Then:** Gainey stood up to the Trump Administration by refusing to collaborate with ICE on unconstitutional raids in the community. And he’s fighting back against Trump’s cuts to vital services like Medicaid and HUD offices. **Now:** Trump allies are spending hundreds of thousands to prop up his opponent and trying to beat Mayor Gainey in the Democratic primary. **The One PA team has been on the ground organizing voters to make sure we can push back against the Trump team's effort to overtake the city.** We have organized 25 precinct captains, built an aggressive door to door program, launched innovative digital programs, and pulled out all the stops. _**[With the primary election tomorrow, we’re pulling out all the stops. Please rush a donation right now to make sure we’re able to finish strong.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107989273/546491486/-551885580?refcode=EM_FL_OPA_20250519_fr_explain_fd_pb_act_xx_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vQ1BELzEvMTEzNTk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQyZGI5OGQzLWU3MzQtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ByRiU1AY8Lr9Bpa7eKU1efCEJcgNT2s0It8q4O1QWZo=)**_ In solidarity, Jeffrey, One PA
---
| One PA is a grassroots movement for racial, economic, and social justice in Pennsylvania. We empower Black communities through voter education, endorse candidates who share our values, and prioritize leadership development. Our commitment is to challenge political disillusionment, protect and expand democracy, and unite everyday people to have a voice in our political system.

And he’s fighting back against Trump’s cuts to vital services like Medicaid and HUD offices. Now: Trump allies are spending hundreds of thousands to prop up his opponent and trying to beat Mayor Gainey in the Democratic primary. The One PA team has been on the ground organizing voters to make sure we can push back against the Trump team's effort to overtake the city. We have organized 25 precinct captains, built an aggressive door to door program, launched innovative digital programs, and pulled out all the stops. = In solidarity, Jeffrey, One PA | One PA is a grassroots movement for racial, economic, and social justice in Pennsylvania. We empower Black communities through voter education, endorse candidates who share our values, and prioritize leadership development. Our commitment is to challenge political disillusionment, protect and expand democracy, and unite everyday people to have a voice in our political system. | = = * = | | Paid for by One Pennsylvania One PA 533 S 52nd St Philadelphia, PA 19143 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Hirono HQ
subject: 14 years ago today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
body:
|
#
---
Ready for a throwback, Peter? Exactly 14 years ago today, on May 19, 2011, Mazie announced her first bid to represent Hawaii in the U.S. Senate! Today, she’s still fighting for working families, for our planet, and for a better future for _**all of us.**_ When she won in 2012, Mazie became the first AANHPI woman, and only the second woman of color, elected to the Senate. This AANHPI Heritage Month, we’re proud to celebrate everything she’s done to elevate the voices of the AANHPI community in Congress. Earlier this month, Mazie teamed up with Rep. Grace Meng to introduce a bicameral resolution officially recognizing May as AANHPI Heritage Month — a powerful reminder that our communities matter and that our stories are integral to America’s story. **Peter, if you’re proud of everything Mazie’s stood for and worked for, help us keep her fighting in the Senate.** **[Will you chip in $10 — or whatever works for you — to help re-elect Mazie and power her continued leadership?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107989566/546492098/-262051991?amount=10&refcode=AU_MKH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250519_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MTU3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTQ3YWM4My1lODM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Merqe0NAqOLDsciQlX1DHgqr4LVRt6nq68v9iJK4MzQ=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $5 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107989567/546492099/-434956623?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=AU_MKH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250519_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MTU3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTQ3YWM4My1lODM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Merqe0NAqOLDsciQlX1DHgqr4LVRt6nq68v9iJK4MzQ=)

clean_body: | Ready for a throwback, Peter? Exactly 14 years ago today, on May 19, 2011, Mazie announced her first bid to represent Hawaii in the U.S. Senate! Today, she’s still fighting for working families, for our planet, and for a better future for all of us. When she won in 2012, Mazie became the first AANHPI woman, and only the second woman of color, elected to the Senate. This AANHPI Heritage Month, we’re proud to celebrate everything she’s done to elevate the voices of the AANHPI community in Congress. Earlier this month, Mazie teamed up with Rep. Grace Meng to introduce a bicameral resolution officially recognizing May as AANHPI Heritage Month — a powerful reminder that our communities matter and that our stories are integral to America’s story. Peter, if you’re proud of everything Mazie’s stood for and worked for, help us keep her fighting in the Senate. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you, — Hirono HQ Friends of Mazie Hirono 335 Merchant Street #677 Honolulu, HI 96809 United States mailto:info@mazieforhawaii.com | Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii

name: Team Gillibrand
subject: The SAVE Act will silence millions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Don’t be fooled by the name. The SAVE Act is an attack on our rights.
The right to vote is a cornerstone in our democracy.

Yet House Republicans have passed the _Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act_ – a bill that would throw up new barriers to voting, especially for women, seniors, young people and communities of color.

Don’t be fooled by the name. If the GOP-held Senate passes the _SAVE Act,_ it will stop millions of Americans from registering to vote by requiring documents many people simply don’t have.

**That’s why we’re calling on the grassroots to voice your opposition to anti-democracy policies like the _SAVE Act_ before it’s too late.**

[**_We are counting on 25,000 grassroots supporters to add their name to our petition SUPPORTING voting rights and OPPOSING voter suppression. Will you be one of them? Sign on today:_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107989630/546492396/862499518?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.05.19_Protect-Voting-Rights_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjUyMzFjYS1lODM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=f6TlT8PzpLzCtNQtNw82XXVJRFx4-hSr9W99WgZPCPY=&emci=c70a5519-d434-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=965231ca-e834-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7659029) | | [SIGN THE PETITION ➞](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107989631/546492397/862499518?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.05.19_Protect-Voting-Rights_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjUyMzFjYS1lODM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=f6TlT8PzpLzCtNQtNw82XXVJRFx4-hSr9W99WgZPCPY=&emci=c70a5519-d434-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=965231ca-e834-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7659029)
---
Here’s what we know:
* **Over 21 million eligible voters** lack the required proof of citizenship under this bill – meaning they could be blocked from voting outright.
* **Almost 70 million women** could face extra hurdles because their birth certificates don’t reflect their legal names after marriage.
* **More than 150 million Americans,** especially in rural communities, don't have a passport and would not be able to register to vote.

The _SAVE Act_ is a targeted attempt to silence women, seniors, people of color and working-class Americans by making voting virtually inaccessible.

Why? _Because Republicans fear the power of a diverse electorate making their voices heard._

[**_We need to act fast to show that we the people REJECT policies that will take away our voting rights. Please be one of the 25,000 supporters we need to sign on before midnight:_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107989632/546492425/862499518?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.05.19_Protect-Voting-Rights_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjUyMzFjYS1lODM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=f6TlT8PzpLzCtNQtNw82XXVJRFx4-hSr9W99WgZPCPY=&emci=c70a5519-d434-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=965231ca-e834-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7659029)

name: Local Democracy Project
subject: There are more of us than them, and we arecoalescing…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Local Democracy Project
body:
| | |
In times of crisis, like-minded organizations unite to safeguard what matters most. As our communities grapple with the weight of Trump’s dystopian agenda, the Local Democracy Project is dedicated to defending our democracy from the ground up.
_Our strategy is clear..._
__Just as Big Ten universities are forming a "[mutual academic defense compact](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.SEgofQhSb9pGg8bz5XQG3XQ6QQ9AGEEMz_7YC6wQDQUbVMpkis9rBDtZI2NgANoEj4VHKqLg7DMFm1L1hIPHG84ibeJ1bWiWCppdZkmnAGwlXHyN71qwGS8ga2xlBDAmqqDMFpkNYO4fEa12W15qbMIH9WYCO09SEKPZKAKxDOYjQYgAltasSB7L1pU2Oz2dTcivcb5dZyzvDrp0eY8V7ZjMMekNTfXGxlJNhV2FeYEpZMkh75ir_-1Cz0yOU8oMQfe9pqAGBhu4R_V1TZmciMQ39ps89FuL0HIrSqoTYJTSZfK8tmM-vc2gD_pvu53kpZuZhpZA5JQAZX7qN5RHJS1Ui0FqzWVMh84DZhU1oa0gO79GOPyZJMMqeYUzeKPsQSrJ8yG1wGdsHaYBnCYB8Q/4gm/ZX-rOtNcSjObALtncpWxeg/h0/h001.r1zIpXRl7SyuKnHjPgpIlpSi0Mhldro8OKH1j1a7KT4)" to shield themselves from MAGA attacks on academic freedom and institutional integrity, we are building formidable coalitions to enhance municipal power and resist authoritarian encroachments. name: USPS Privatization Alert via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Save USPS
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

BREAKING: Donald Trump has picked a top executive from FedEx to be the
next Postmaster General – paving the way for privatization of the United
States Postal Service.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/27097?source=email-20250519v&t=1031&akid=8497%2E5885482%2EgbxzVG ]Headline: 'Aggresive Step' Toward Privatization as Trump Picks FedEx
Board Member to Lead USPS

It's critically urgent we act NOW. There is a bipartisan resolution in the
Senate opposing USPS privatization — and every member of Congress needs to
hear from us, loudly and clearly, that it is completely unacceptable to
privatize USPS.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/27098?source=email-20250519v&t=1032&akid=8497%2E5885482%2EgbxzVG ]Tell Congress: Oppose Trump's dangerous plan — keep the Postal Service
public and accountable to the American people!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/27098?source=email-20250519v&t=1033&akid=8497%2E5885482%2EgbxzVG ]ADD YOUR NAME

The United States Postal Service isn't just any agency—it's a lifeline
connecting rural Americans, seniors, veterans, small businesses, and
low-income families. Unlike private delivery companies, USPS is legally
obligated to provide affordable, reliable service to every address in
America, no matter how remote or costly the route.

Privatization would mean higher prices, less service, and a devastating
blow to the dedicated USPS workers who deliver essential medical
prescriptions, small business products, senior benefit checks, mail-in
ballots, and vital community communications – and the Our Revolution
Citizen’s Army has been on the streets with our partners the American
Postal Workers Union to stop this nightmare.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/27097?source=email-20250519v&t=1034&akid=8497%2E5885482%2EgbxzVG ]Photo from the USPS protests

The pick of David Steiner, director of FedEx, a direct competitor to USPS,
could at last fulfill Trump’s decade-long desire to punish the Postal
Service through hostile takeover. We have to mobilize everyone and
everything we’ve got to protect USPS from Trump.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/27098?source=email-20250519v&t=1035&akid=8497%2E5885482%2EgbxzVG ]Add your name to join thousands of Our Revolution members calling on
Congress to reject privatization and protect USPS from Trump!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/27098?source=email-20250519v&t=1036&akid=8497%2E5885482%2EgbxzVG ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win,

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

name: Former FBI Special Agent Mike Rogers
subject: << For your eyes only >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
PLEASE make sure you’re in a private spot when you open this.

Psst.

# [The Cruelty of Medicaid Cuts](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=163923483&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzOTIzNDgzLCJpYXQiOjE3NDc2ODQ4OTIsImV4cCI6MTc1MDI3Njg5MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.S_pL4_J9ZwzenSaQLLQQlrcXKLfdmugLUQy6VBVmKy4)
### What you can do right now (for free) to protect care for tens of millions of people
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| May 19
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)

---

Nationwide nearly 80 million Americans rely on Medicaid for critical healthcare. It supports newborns and aging adults, disabled people and caregivers, rural communities and urban centers. It ensures that children get their check-ups, that aging adults can age in place, and that disabled people can live with dignity. But right now, that vital lifeline is under attack. And given that last night the House passed this cruel budget cut out of committee, we have even less time to act. Here's a breakdown of where we are, and what we can do to stop these horrendous cuts from becoming law. **Let 's Address This.**
Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/96027d52-a379-4ec3-afe9-ca40c3b069db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## Here's The Current Situation
Congressional Republicans are planning at least $715 billion in cuts to Medicaid. Why? To bankroll what the non-partisan Congressional Budget Office estimates as $5.5 trillion dollars in tax breaks for billionaires and the largest corporations. These cuts aren't just numbers on a spreadsheet. They represent a catastrophic rollback of care for our most vulnerable. Let's not mince words. If these cuts happen, people will die.
Part of the strategy to fight back is to ensure people truly understand just how devastating these cuts are, and how these cuts will affect their lives on a daily basis. So, I've done my best to synthesize some of the most significant cuts and subsequent harms they will cause.
## **What These Cuts Will Do**
1. **Rip healthcare away from millions** : The Congressional Republican Bill will rip health care away from nearly 14 million people, with millions of seniors, children, disabled people, and families losing coverage. For low-income families already struggling, this could be the difference between life and death.
2. **Devastate rural communities** : Medicaid funds many rural hospitals and clinics. Without it, entire regions could lose access to emergency and routine care. Rural hospitals will shut down and doctors will leave rural areas altogether.
3. **Destroy care for disabled people** : Remember, Americans with disabilities live in every neighborhood in every community in this country, including more than 1 in 4 adults. With Medicaid slashed, at least 7.8 million aging adults and disabled people who rely on Medicaid for home and community-based services that provide things like in-home care, transportation, and personal assistance will be harmed. Cuts mean longer waitlists (already 700,000 people long), deeper isolation, and will leave people without critical support that enable living and aging with dignity
4. **Undermine our workforce** : Medicaid pays the wages of 4.8 million care workers. Cuts would slash their pay, drive them from the field, potentially take their health care away, and worsen the care crisis. Hospitals and other medical providers would be pushed to close, both losing hundreds of thousands of health care jobs and losing access to care. Businesses of all kinds would likewise suffer because workers who are sick or injured would not be able to get the care they need in a timely manner.
5. **Punish caregivers and women** : Medicaid supports family caregivers with respite and mental health services in addition to basic healthcare. Without it, more unpaid caregivers will burn out--especially women, who already do the lion's share of care work.

## **The Danger Goes Beyond Cuts**
One of the most dangerous ideas on the table is so-called "work requirements." These bureaucratic hurdles would force people to prove they're working to receive care--even if they're already employed, disabled, in school, or caregiving. This would lead to millions of people losing coverage because of confusing paperwork and red tape--not because they're ineligible. Republicans already tried this in Arkansas as recently as 2018. In that failed experiment, 18,000 eligible people lost coverage and the state saw no increase in employment.
Work requirements don't help people work--but Medicaid does. It provides the stability and services people need to stay healthy enough to work, study, and care for loved ones.
## **A Moral and Economic Catastrophe**
Medicaid is not a handout. It's an investment in human dignity. It keeps people in their homes. It supports small businesses by covering workers. It saves money by preventing costly ER visits. And it's popular--more than 3 in 4 voters support it, and bipartisan majorities oppose cuts.
If Congress succeeds in gutting Medicaid, the consequences will ripple through every facet of American life. Hospitals will close. Care workers will vanish. Families will go bankrupt from medical debt. People's lives will be at risk. Denying care doesn't just hurt--it can kill. We cannot let that happen.
## **What You Can Do**
We don't have to accept this. We are not helpless. We have the power to fight back:
1. **Call Your Member of Congress** : Dial 866-426-2631 and demand they oppose Medicaid cuts. Share your story.
2. **Email Your Lawmaker** : Use this easy tool:[ caring.nu/protect-medicaid-2025](https://substack.com/redirect/ee22f4eb-3c9b-48ac-a947-990e80bd4ad9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Tell them care matters more than tax breaks for billionaires.
3. **Share Your Story** : On social media, with your local press, or at town halls. Let your community and lawmakers know what Medicaid means to you.
4. **Build Community:** I am doing my best to raise awareness at every opportunity. Subscribe below to stay up to date on the latest developments and calls to action.

This isn't about party lines. This is not about left vs right. It's about our shared humanity. Whether you're a disabled person, parent, caregiver, worker, or someone who just believes in justice--this fight is yours.
Congress may have power, but we have people. Let's remind them who they work for.
**Before you go! I am doing everything in my power to uphold justice in the United States, and I need your support. Subscribe and join the movement.**

For low-income families already struggling, this could be the difference between life and death. 2. Devastate rural communities : Medicaid funds many rural hospitals and clinics. Without it, entire regions could lose access to emergency and routine care. Rural hospitals will shut down and doctors will leave rural areas altogether. 3. Destroy care for disabled people : Remember, Americans with disabilities live in every neighborhood in every community in this country, including more than 1 in 4 adults. With Medicaid slashed, at least 7.8 million aging adults and disabled people who rely on Medicaid for home and community-based services that provide things like in-home care, transportation, and personal assistance will be harmed. Cuts mean longer waitlists (already 700,000 people long), deeper isolation, and will leave people without critical support that enable living and aging with dignity 4. Undermine our workforce : Medicaid pays the wages of 4.8 million care workers. Cuts would slash their pay, drive them from the field, potentially take their health care away, and worsen the care crisis. Hospitals and other medical providers would be pushed to close, both losing hundreds of thousands of health care jobs and losing access to care. Businesses of all kinds would likewise suffer because workers who are sick or injured would not be able to get the care they need in a timely manner. 5. Punish caregivers and women : Medicaid supports family caregivers with respite and mental health services in addition to basic healthcare. Without it, more unpaid caregivers will burn out--especially women, who already do the lion's share of care work. The Danger Goes Beyond Cuts One of the most dangerous ideas on the table is so-called "work requirements." These bureaucratic hurdles would force people to prove they're working to receive care--even if they're already employed, disabled, in school, or caregiving. This would lead to millions of people losing coverage because of confusing paperwork and red tape--not because they're ineligible. Republicans already tried this in Arkansas as recently as 2018. In that failed experiment, 18,000 eligible people lost coverage and the state saw no increase in employment. Work requirements don't help people work--but Medicaid does. It provides the stability and services people need to stay healthy enough to work, study, and care for loved ones. A Moral and Economic Catastrophe Medicaid is not a handout. It's an investment in human dignity. It keeps people in their homes. It supports small businesses by covering workers. It saves money by preventing costly ER visits. And it's popular--more than 3 in 4 voters support it, and bipartisan majorities oppose cuts. If Congress succeeds in gutting Medicaid, the consequences will ripple through every facet of American life. Hospitals will close. Care workers will vanish. Families will go bankrupt from medical debt. People's lives will be at risk. Denying care doesn't just hurt--it can kill. We cannot let that happen. What You Can Do We don't have to accept this. We are not helpless. We have the power to fight back: 1. Call Your Member of Congress : Dial 866-426-2631 and demand they oppose Medicaid cuts. Share your story. 2. Email Your Lawmaker : Use this easy tool:. Tell them care matters more than tax breaks for billionaires. 3. Share Your Story : On social media, with your local press, or at town halls. Let your community and lawmakers know what Medicaid means to you. 4. Build Community: I am doing my best to raise awareness at every opportunity. Subscribe below to stay up to date on the latest developments and calls to action. This isn't about party lines. This is not about left vs right. It's about our shared humanity. Whether you're a disabled person, parent, caregiver, worker, or someone who just believes in justice--this fight is yours. Congress may have power, but we have people. Let's remind them who they work for. Before you go! I am doing everything in my power to uphold justice in the United States, and I need your support. Subscribe and join the movement. | | ---|---|--- Quick Ask! I am doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support.Become a paid subscriber and join the movement.** | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Qasim Rashid, Esq. 1552 South Route 59 #1333 Naperville, IL 60564 ? ?

Although it might seem early, we need to set the tone for re-election now. We’re one of the only races in the country already rated as a toss-up by experts at the _Cook Political Report_ , _Inside Elections_ , and _Sabato’s Crystal Ball_.

I’m in Congress fighting day and night for CA-45 – working for you, not turning to special interests like Big Pharma or the NRA to keep this seat blue. **[So if we’re going to keep this seat for CA-45 and control of the House, it has to be together: Can I count on your signature of endorsement today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107990359/546495317/387751194?refcode=em-5.19.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODFlN2UzZGMtZWIzNC1mMDExLWE1ZjEtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ElYk_tyK_KwNvQ_EhSAjmz2CyO99--fiZiyv1JGEznA=&emci=1d8fc534-b831-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=81e7e3dc-eb34-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=27336212)**

name: AG Jason Miyares
subject: Freedom or failure: the choice this November
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Miyares for Virginia |
body:
On my watch, Virginia will NOT go backwards.| | | Friend, I have spent the last four years protecting our families, our kids, and our freedoms— _and I have delivered real results:_

**_✅ 30% drop in statewide murders_**
** _✅ $1.1 billion from Big Pharma to fight addiction _**
**_✅ 340,500 kids protected with Child ID kits _**
**_✅ Fentanyl deaths down 44% — _one of the largest drops in the nation__**

But far-left radicals, like **Shannon Taylor** and **Jay Jones,** are lining up to dismantle all of it.

Jones, who will be _“more activist and liberal”_ as Attorney General, voted against prosecuting fentanyl dealers who poison and kill Americans.

And Taylor? She calls herself a _“progressive prosecutor”_ , gutted cash bail, and refuses to hold criminals accountable even in the worst cases.

I stand with law enforcement. I stand with parents and children. I stand with victims. I stand with **YOU,** friend.

But I cannot win this fight alone. [_**Will you chip in to help me meet my May goal and ensure that we defend the progress we have made?**_](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/s/c/k-aEBt7zCyHAuu31enypzjm9jzn1WB-0DaydtRQpShKOPtYrM_gAspvEp5tt6Bpble_A6IzUONHyLfY8_31QPkN7rEQws8MQDlaHrIi14TV-pdmBY7acdvWA2Ye2CquUv42s7RBYYR3cVSgRIIU5eM2QdvUCpS8EAvz0yDyXoYvYFPDuw7oIFFFGIW-fuUjRQ8IikDTBaQ9le6yshf_DhAd9N5imkEjWEb0zCTMAwMVq4CN2VseMmL2-_cAFkdlhv8QgW8DJ1-vYh6IQJ4G4oDqyBClpNEsGre65Ks56lq8JbGpjknhywcA9RqOihbsRmSSZLOxCA4yE4_MIwTexGTsMbLSkN09-Vnu_VcrdlF9yn9mE9dCkEXTPbFA/hHX9sB0rruWSHaNaPxQ6JgjU2bECRoCH/9)

clean_body: On my watch, Virginia will NOT go backwards.| | | Friend, I have spent the last four years protecting our families, our kids, and our freedoms— and I have delivered real results: ✅ 30% drop in statewide murders ✅ $1.1 billion from Big Pharma to fight addiction ✅ 340,500 kids protected with Child ID kits ✅ Fentanyl deaths down 44% — one of the largest drops in the nation But far-left radicals, like Shannon Taylor and Jay Jones, are lining up to dismantle all of it. Jones, who will be “more activist and liberal” as Attorney General, voted against prosecuting fentanyl dealers who poison and kill Americans. And Taylor? She calls herself a “progressive prosecutor” , gutted cash bail, and refuses to hold criminals accountable even in the worst cases. I stand with law enforcement. I stand with parents and children. I stand with victims. I stand with YOU, friend. But I cannot win this fight alone. On my watch, Virginia will not go backwards. For Virginia, Jason Miyares | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | Paid for by Miyares for Virginia | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

name: Tom Barrett
subject: Become a Barrett Brigade Volunteer!
body:
There’s a parade this weekend | | | [](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=682b8e085863483d5fcd2271&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftombarrettforcongress.com%2F%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7%26ss_email_id%3D682b8e085863483d5fcd2271%26ss_campaign_name%3DBecome%2Ba%2BBarrett%2BBrigade%2BVolunteer%2521%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-05-19T20%253A01%253A48Z&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7&l=en-US&s=vmtGqhh3DqVaoB9bkp6u2hJOUCA%3D)
---
| Peter Willis,I’m gearing up for one of the biggest congressional races in America. Republicans hold a narrow majority and the only path for Democrats requires them defeating me in 2026.[**I’m hoping I can ask you if you’d be willing to help my campaign as a volunteer?**](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=682b8e085863483d5fcd2271&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftombarrettforcongress.com%2Fget-involved%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7%26ss_email_id%3D682b8e085863483d5fcd2271%26ss_campaign_name%3DBecome%2Ba%2BBarrett%2BBrigade%2BVolunteer%2521%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-05-19T20%253A01%253A48Z&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7&l=en-US&s=wRB5nGQhMXwgFTujnhBGzaroq6Y%3D)
---
[_ _**VOLUNTEER** _ _](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=682b8e085863483d5fcd2271&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftombarrettforcongress.com%2Fget-involved%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7%26ss_email_id%3D682b8e085863483d5fcd2271%26ss_campaign_name%3DBecome%2Ba%2BBarrett%2BBrigade%2BVolunteer%2521%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-05-19T20%253A01%253A48Z&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7&l=en-US&s=wRB5nGQhMXwgFTujnhBGzaroq6Y%3D)
---
It doesn’t require a ton of time this early out, but there’s many fun things that you can join me and my family doing for the campaign.Beginning this Saturday, we have a heavy schedule of parades. We’ve always been surrounded by dozens of supporters at these parades and now that I am in Congress, it is even more important we get out into the community.[And many other ways to help.](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=682b8e085863483d5fcd2271&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftombarrettforcongress.com%2Fget-involved%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7%26ss_email_id%3D682b8e085863483d5fcd2271%26ss_campaign_name%3DBecome%2Ba%2BBarrett%2BBrigade%2BVolunteer%2521%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-05-19T20%253A01%253A48Z&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7&l=en-US&s=wRB5nGQhMXwgFTujnhBGzaroq6Y%3D)
---
|
---
| _**The Barrett Brigade at a 2024 parade.**_
---
[_ _**VOLUNTEER** _ _](https://a1e0.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=682b8e085863483d5fcd2271&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftombarrettforcongress.com%2Fget-involved%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7%26ss_email_id%3D682b8e085863483d5fcd2271%26ss_campaign_name%3DBecome%2Ba%2BBarrett%2BBrigade%2BVolunteer%2521%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2025-05-19T20%253A01%253A48Z&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_682b7f5a30064e654e0171c7&l=en-US&s=wRB5nGQhMXwgFTujnhBGzaroq6Y%3D)
---
I’m proud of the work I’ve done since getting sworn in just five months ago. We’ve done so much for the district already and I’m working hard with my colleagues to reduce wasteful spending and cutting our deficit and $37 trillion debt.Will you sign up to be a volunteer today? Click this link and sign up on my webpage and my campaign team will follow-up.I’ve been endorsed by President Trump and Speaker of the House Mike Johnson. My race has been singled out as one of the most competitive in the nation and a top target for national Democrats. Please consider volunteering and protecting our Republican majority.
---
|
---
| | |
---|---|---
PS - Even if you were signed up as a volunteer last year, please sign-up again so we are sure we have all your contact information and the best way to get you involved.
---

name: In This Together PAC HQ
subject: It's time to rein our democracy back in.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
"The job of government is to serve the American people"
Peter,

Right now, Republicans are using their power not to fix the government — **but to break it.**

They’re gutting programs like Medicaid, slashing the Child Tax Credit, and dismantling protections that keep families safe, healthy, and cared for — from affordable housing to nutrition programs for kids. At the same time, they’re pushing harmful tariffs that raise prices on working families, attacking labor protections, and undermining environmental safeguards.

But Governor Andy Beshear is fighting back. Andy knows that the government is built by the people and should serve the people, but extremists in Washington are doing everything they can to break that system. clean_body: "The job of government is to serve the American people" Peter, Right now, Republicans are using their power not to fix the government — but to break it. They’re gutting programs like Medicaid, slashing the Child Tax Credit, and dismantling protections that keep families safe, healthy, and cared for — from affordable housing to nutrition programs for kids. At the same time, they’re pushing harmful tariffs that raise prices on working families, attacking labor protections, and undermining environmental safeguards. But Governor Andy Beshear is fighting back. Andy knows that the government is built by the people and should serve the people, but extremists in Washington are doing everything they can to break that system. = Now, it’s time to rein it back in. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Onward, In This Together PAC HQ | Andy and In This Together PAC are working to support leaders who will lead with compassion and decency, reject anger politics, and always do what's right. body:
As a combat-proven commander, I know the early stages of any campaign can set the tempo for an entire battle. |
[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=6a7ae8d2a1eb4b7cad0ccf00df96f47e&_e=HITPhmvx31RveEjaQ0zxbc-3U_MZrm51Z1MFz4MkopKAUvpMqMXmc19bsbEr4D5anmLnVImZM6SwEB7EgrDYdaqrXQGb9y_EJ5L0JNBndaO107AnMgQbbqdXVMXy8skS0o4bn8AqStBwAtuAlWaaSIdMH2g90pbaSTfFmsNlnOHV87aRIav0SpG7L9FoK0ECFZ9LhpcHhMul2L_49rQhWZaf7htZ89t-MQJAzd7P6dq-HaAb8tkbWEuWeW7oB2dGuRJXgSYevBI0oZd5zL9JgGuv9qulavR5KOPGxEwheCRyfoHRg5uCsqv1fHELdYgwebLSfN7n5waOC1wuWTNuYNZcwy9idEyZAIq1KNnoVzlc0J3tMoHtUcR4O-FA0gwGmxDnssjnD4oXKgvBD71i0A%3D%3D)

---
Fellow Conservative,

As a combat-proven commander, I know the early stages of any campaign can set the tempo for an entire battle.

I started my campaign to flip the country’s MOST contested Senate seat (Georgia) **RED** last Monday. **The money we raise in this first week will have a huge impact on how this campaign takes shape.**

_**My Democrat opponent, Jon Ossoff, has already shattered fundraising records, raising more than $11 million in the first three months of the year**_. No doubt, he’s already raised millions more, too.

We must come out swinging in the **_first week_. It’s the only way to guarantee our campaign has what it takes to stay on offense.**

By hitting this goal, we’ll put Jon Ossoff and the Democrats on notice, and our campaign will have the resources it needs to keep building momentum.

[**I need to know right now that I can count on your support to push through my first fundraising objective of this campaign. name: Dr. Greg Murphy
subject: My story
disclaimer_text: Paid for by friends to elect Dr
body:
I truly believe that God called on me to assist others during their times of hardship because he knew that I could carry the burdens of those I deliver aid to, improving their lives tremendously. | [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=a4f0b3a6abae41de964ca1a9731817ed&_e=7rMFd79GxIjxD45xRoPMxxy5BZdU5LCfqVYt2GX9-2iMkGnF2dmDzVVj0Ibd0jKifNnWRikaMeH3rnzQX_nZD0nFKC14zvH32PYuVDZqSRPWdz-CpMZ-8V7YIEZP3CMf0wiqeB22hm0ahtZCABUpIUFgPLJJc7AIxWc7gQDPwV33zno_s3O-gWUfZhOk_QfCdajlCqWQmy_C4oY9CMXce-1ub5uUHCyOwW5EE9FWgusPPots20Ng0E36cbDP3Nqpp9tn_qCr3bI4wkezb5Avz8uiZ_qMR4Tapw4tJlS3Zo4xuX55KmhAKeHR8UzHx3D672FlHycgWqJeRgxizpE3ykS0a_XAn-tnuV7tb7eV2Z4BrltoEXE9koBAGmws56eGbR6YJ8MMjSY41Sy1_03VRA%3D%3D)

---

Peter,

Do you know why I decided to become a doctor?

It’s because I knew that many people across the world and within our great country **desperately needed help**. I truly believe that God called on me to assist others during their times of hardship because he knew that I could carry the burdens of those I deliver aid to, improving their lives tremendously.

As a result, the past 35 years of my life have been spent in the medical field.

I graduated magna cum laude from Davidson College in 1985 and then graduated with honors from the UNC School of Medicine. I completed a surgical residency in Urology and Renal Transplantation at the University of Kentucky in Lexington and then opened up my own practice in Greenville, North Carolina, after moving there with my wife over 20 years ago.

I’ve traveled far and wide to places such as India, several parts of Africa, Nicaragua and Haiti as a Medical Missionary, serving both God and people from all walks of life. During this time, I was fortunate enough to meet Mother Teresa, who further inspired me to serve those in need.

My endeavors to lend a helping hand did not stop there. When Congressman Walter B. Jones, Jr. passed away in 2019 after 22 years of service and North Carolina was left without someone to represent their needs, **I felt called on to step up.**

I have been blessed to be able to serve the great people of North Carolina in Congress. Although this journey is a new one, I have learned it is not so different from working in the medical field. _**People want someone to care about their needs and take action to fix the issues that are afflicting them.**_

I excel as a congressman because I know how to diagnose people’s concerns and work with my colleagues that will achieve results.

It has been a privilege to represent the wonderful people of North Carolina, _but I’m not ready to give up on this position just yet_. I wish to proceed with my duties and continue work on issues that are important to you.

For me to fulfill my earthly responsibility and keep North Carolina on course for prosperity, I must implore you to do something for me. [**Will you please stand behind me with your financial support?**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=a4f0b3a6abae41de964ca1a9731817ed&_e=7rMFd79GxIjxD45xRoPMxxy5BZdU5LCfqVYt2GX9-2iMkGnF2dmDzVVj0Ibd0jKifNnWRikaMeH3rnzQX_nZD0nFKC14zvH32PYuVDZqSRPWdz-CpMZ-8V7YIEZP3CMf0wiqeB22hm0ahtZCABUpIUFgPLJJc7AIxWc7gQDPwV33zno_s3O-gWUfZhOk_QfCdajlCqWQmy_C4oY9CMXce-1ub5uUHCyOwW5EE9FWgusPPots20Ng0E36cbDP3Nqpbo314xbd1rVKMZKTazv-E-OCdb0ff9IpLA8jOEmJ-tXUfF_K6lyUfz1ml433VypRWFjM0cV_8gSfNBLc5g2aweN7aXI7jke6IUe-FazX_p69eV178d3NtbtxrriioQlcTxYlMZoVj44qwJ1FZbynPQ%3D%3D)

[**STAND WITH GREG ->**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=a4f0b3a6abae41de964ca1a9731817ed&_e=7rMFd79GxIjxD45xRoPMxxy5BZdU5LCfqVYt2GX9-2iMkGnF2dmDzVVj0Ibd0jKifNnWRikaMeH3rnzQX_nZD0nFKC14zvH32PYuVDZqSRPWdz-CpMZ-8V7YIEZP3CMf0wiqeB22hm0ahtZCABUpIUFgPLJJc7AIxWc7gQDPwV33zno_s3O-gWUfZhOk_QfCdajlCqWQmy_C4oY9CMXce-1ub5uUHCyOwW5EE9FWgusPPots20Ng0E36cbDP3NqpAYw4oNamozkI-emvl_wniuHE_13GdspNMuV7Jl2BDq_O4VGV1CJ_KDQHwiuzRk3JBr8zHX8fXfL33vPW588Q4R2MqPqashdFXBCVMWD-JUqyClpdIM8BTyS5mfETEmJJWtnhLqdp-IMD0NMCUYd1qw%3D%3D)
I am aware that what I’m asking may be difficult, but it is necessary – especially if we want to continue our hard work. It’s my calling to serve you, [**but I can’t do that if Democrats retake the House Majority and block our America First legislation.**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=a4f0b3a6abae41de964ca1a9731817ed&_e=7rMFd79GxIjxD45xRoPMxxy5BZdU5LCfqVYt2GX9-2iMkGnF2dmDzVVj0Ibd0jKifNnWRikaMeH3rnzQX_nZD0nFKC14zvH32PYuVDZqSRPWdz-CpMZ-8V7YIEZP3CMf0wiqeB22hm0ahtZCABUpIUFgPLJJc7AIxWc7gQDPwV33zno_s3O-gWUfZhOk_QfCdajlCqWQmy_C4oY9CMXce-1ub5uUHCyOwW5EE9FWgusPPots20Ng0E36cbDP3Nqp77QjMHlT0tNRIOpSpguBpLs6X8nlTq8Y44nAEwqptLIfbVe00WRzPy72KHDNZ7AbHdoOJ99I4PKfB9x0BkR1hZDQBUm7YA5N2TTo8efL2eyAtY2Bpb_V_zGKIcoG6WFxOb685HAUzsBQQka6qjT_GQ%3D%3D)

As a devout member of the church and a trusted physician within my community, I can promise that I will continue to selflessly represent you in Congress. I will continue to advocate for your needs and champion North Carolina values, just as I advocate for my patients and for the word of God.

clean_body: I truly believe that God called on me to assist others during their times of hardship because he knew that I could carry the burdens of those I deliver aid to, improving their lives tremendously. | Peter, Do you know why I decided to become a doctor? It’s because I knew that many people across the world and within our great country desperately needed help. I truly believe that God called on me to assist others during their times of hardship because he knew that I could carry the burdens of those I deliver aid to, improving their lives tremendously. As a result, the past 35 years of my life have been spent in the medical field. I graduated magna cum laude from Davidson College in 1985 and then graduated with honors from the UNC School of Medicine. I completed a surgical residency in Urology and Renal Transplantation at the University of Kentucky in Lexington and then opened up my own practice in Greenville, North Carolina, after moving there with my wife over 20 years ago. I’ve traveled far and wide to places such as India, several parts of Africa, Nicaragua and Haiti as a Medical Missionary, serving both God and people from all walks of life. During this time, I was fortunate enough to meet Mother Teresa, who further inspired me to serve those in need. My endeavors to lend a helping hand did not stop there. When Congressman Walter B. Jones, Jr. passed away in 2019 after 22 years of service and North Carolina was left without someone to represent their needs, I felt called on to step up. I have been blessed to be able to serve the great people of North Carolina in Congress. Although this journey is a new one, I have learned it is not so different from working in the medical field. People want someone to care about their needs and take action to fix the issues that are afflicting them. I excel as a congressman because I know how to diagnose people’s concerns and work with my colleagues that will achieve results. It has been a privilege to represent the wonderful people of North Carolina, but I’m not ready to give up on this position just yet. I wish to proceed with my duties and continue work on issues that are important to you. For me to fulfill my earthly responsibility and keep North Carolina on course for prosperity, I must implore you to do something for me. I am aware that what I’m asking may be difficult, but it is necessary – especially if we want to continue our hard work. It’s my calling to serve you, As a devout member of the church and a trusted physician within my community, I can promise that I will continue to selflessly represent you in Congress. I will continue to advocate for your needs and champion North Carolina values, just as I advocate for my patients and for the word of God. Together, I know we can build a nation affluent in conservative values. Thank you, Dr. Greg Murphy (R-NC 3) | | Paid for by friends to elect Dr. Greg Murphy to Congress Murphy for Congress P.O. Box 1131 Greenville, North Carolina 27835 Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but .

name: Eric H. Holder, Jr.
subject: A journey, not a destination
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
A reminder that progress is possible, that justice can prevail, and that each generation has a responsibility to push our nation closer to its highest ideals.
|
---

Peter,
Seventy-one years ago this month, in May, 1954, nine justices came together and unanimously declared that separate was inherently unequal.
The Supreme Court's decision in _Brown v. Board of Education_ was more than a legal ruling — it brought the law in line with the fundamental truth of the equality of our humanity.
**Yet, we must acknowledge that the promise of _Brown_ , as well as the civil rights movement as a whole, has been — and continues to be — a journey, not a destination.**
The integration of our schools was halting, confrontational, and at times even bloody. The outlawing of institutional segregation did not by itself alleviate the vicious bias that had been directed against African American people and communities for generations. And the rejection of legal discrimination by our highest court could not by itself wash away the hostility that would, for years, fuel perversely innovative attempts to keep "separate but equal" in place.
These markers of progress could not forestall the "Massive Resistance" policies that followed across the country. Public schools were closed, and private academies were opened for white children only. The Little Rock Nine required the protection of the 101st Airborne Division of the United States Army to enroll in an all-white high school.
And they could not prevent Alabama Governor George Wallace from making his infamous “Stand in the Schoolhouse Door” nine years after _Brown_ , when two courageous African American students attempted to register for classes at the University of Alabama — one of whom, Vivian Malone, would much later become my sister-in-law.
But thanks to _Brown_ — and to the developments that followed — when Vivian and her classmate James Hood walked into that university, they were protected not only by the power of their convictions, but by the strength of the National Guard, the authority of the United States Department of Justice, and the force of binding law.
When those nine students entered Little Rock Central High School, they were supported by all nine members of a resolute Supreme Court. And when millions of civil rights advocates and supporters began to rally, to march, to stand up — and even to sit in — to eradicate the discrimination they continued to face in schools and other public accommodations, they stood not only on the side of equality and what was right, but on the side of settled justice.
This was the sea change that _Brown_ signaled. It did not instantaneously or painlessly tear down the walls that divided our nation. But it did unlock the gates.
**The story of _Brown_ is not just a historical narrative, but a continuing call to action. It reminds us that progress is possible, that justice can prevail, and that each generation has a responsibility to push our nation closer to its highest ideals.**
**We should also remember that _Brown’s_ impact extended beyond schools. By saying that segregation was unacceptable, it laid the foundation for future accomplishments in the civil rights movement — accomplishments that we must still protect.**
You can draw a direct line between _Brown_ and the Voting Rights Act of 1965, which finally created a robust safeguard against discrimination and disenfranchisement in our elections, and which established strong tools to deliver on the promise of fair representation with fair district lines.
But anti-democracy forces are now seeking to weaken the Voting Rights Act — in a continued, concerted effort to achieve unearned, illegitimate power at the expense of people of color.
So as we mark this 71st anniversary of _Brown_ , let us recommit ourselves to upholding the values it represents and dismantling the barriers that still divide us — by fighting for equal rights, equal representation through fair maps, and the fundamental promise that every voice matters in our democracy.
Sincerely,
Eric H. Holder, Jr.
82nd Attorney General of the United States

clean_body: A reminder that progress is possible, that justice can prevail, and that each generation has a responsibility to push our nation closer to its highest ideals. | Peter, Seventy-one years ago this month, in May, 1954, nine justices came together and unanimously declared that separate was inherently unequal. The Supreme Court's decision in Brown v. Board of Education was more than a legal ruling — it brought the law in line with the fundamental truth of the equality of our humanity. Yet, we must acknowledge that the promise of Brown , as well as the civil rights movement as a whole, has been — and continues to be — a journey, not a destination. The integration of our schools was halting, confrontational, and at times even bloody. The outlawing of institutional segregation did not by itself alleviate the vicious bias that had been directed against African American people and communities for generations. And the rejection of legal discrimination by our highest court could not by itself wash away the hostility that would, for years, fuel perversely innovative attempts to keep "separate but equal" in place. These markers of progress could not forestall the "Massive Resistance" policies that followed across the country. Public schools were closed, and private academies were opened for white children only. The Little Rock Nine required the protection of the 101st Airborne Division of the United States Army to enroll in an all-white high school. And they could not prevent Alabama Governor George Wallace from making his infamous “Stand in the Schoolhouse Door” nine years after Brown , when two courageous African American students attempted to register for classes at the University of Alabama — one of whom, Vivian Malone, would much later become my sister-in-law. But thanks to Brown — and to the developments that followed — when Vivian and her classmate James Hood walked into that university, they were protected not only by the power of their convictions, but by the strength of the National Guard, the authority of the United States Department of Justice, and the force of binding law. When those nine students entered Little Rock Central High School, they were supported by all nine members of a resolute Supreme Court. And when millions of civil rights advocates and supporters began to rally, to march, to stand up — and even to sit in — to eradicate the discrimination they continued to face in schools and other public accommodations, they stood not only on the side of equality and what was right, but on the side of settled justice. This was the sea change that Brown signaled. It did not instantaneously or painlessly tear down the walls that divided our nation. But it did unlock the gates. The story of Brown is not just a historical narrative, but a continuing call to action. It reminds us that progress is possible, that justice can prevail, and that each generation has a responsibility to push our nation closer to its highest ideals. We should also remember that Brown’s impact extended beyond schools. By saying that segregation was unacceptable, it laid the foundation for future accomplishments in the civil rights movement — accomplishments that we must still protect. You can draw a direct line between Brown and the Voting Rights Act of 1965, which finally created a robust safeguard against discrimination and disenfranchisement in our elections, and which established strong tools to deliver on the promise of fair representation with fair district lines. But anti-democracy forces are now seeking to weaken the Voting Rights Act — in a continued, concerted effort to achieve unearned, illegitimate power at the expense of people of color. So as we mark this 71st anniversary of Brown , let us recommit ourselves to upholding the values it represents and dismantling the barriers that still divide us — by fighting for equal rights, equal representation through fair maps, and the fundamental promise that every voice matters in our democracy. Sincerely, Eric H. Holder, Jr. 82nd Attorney General of the United States is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. And that's just March. Their quarterly total?
$36.7 million last quarter.
Republicans aren’t just hoping to hold the House. They’re planning to straight-up purchase it.
The NRCC is quietly flooding battleground districts with dark money, billionaire cash, and far-right talking points — all in an effort to lock in GOP control of Congress before most voters have even tuned in.
We must match their war chest dollar for dollar - and thanks to the strength of our grassroots movement, we can.
